title: Bacterial Nucleic Acid Extraction Protocol Adapted for a Resource-Limited Setting
author: Bonie Datul

steps_raw.instruction: Inoculate a loopful of bacteria in 5 mL of Nutrient Broth. Incubate Overnight at Room temperature with shaking.
Transfer 1.5 mL of the overnight Sample into a sterile fresh 1.5-mL microcentrifuge tube.
Harvest the cells by centrifuge at 12.000 rpm, °C, for 00:03:00 . Carefully pour off or pipette out the supernatant, leaving the cell pellet.
Resuspend the bacterial cell pellet with 150 µL Resuspension Buffer. Pipette up and down vigorously to disperse the cells. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 .

Add 10 µL Proteinase K ( 20 mg/mL ) to the suspension. Vortex to mix well. Incubate at 65 °C for 00:20:00 , vortex gently every 5 minutes. Lysate should be clear at the end of incubation. Otherwise, extend the incubation time to 30 minutes or until the lysate clarifies.
Cool the lysate to room temperature. Then add 35.0 µL of 5.0 Molarity (M) NaCl solution (approximately 0.5 Molarity (M) final salt concentration).
Mix thoroughly by inverting the tube 10-20 times. Or vortex gently for 5seconds, to avoid shearing of DNA.
Incubate On ice for 00:10:00 . (Critical in the precipitation of proteins and cell debris).

Centrifuge at 14.000 rpm, °C, for 00:03:00 . A white pellet (protein and cell debris) should be visible at the bottom. A white pellet (protein and cell debris) should be visible at the bottom of the tube. If not, place the tube on ice for 5-10 minutes. After which, the pellets should be observable.
Carefully transfer 250 µL of the supernatant (which contains the DNA) to a new, clean 1.5-mL tube. Be careful not to transfer any of the white protein pellet!

Add 500 µL cold 70% ethanol to the tube. Mix gently by inverting the tube a few times to wash the DNA. Centrifuge at 14.000 rpm, °C, for 00:03:00 (at 4 °C , if available). Tip: Orient tubes in an equal fashion to facilitate subsequent removal of supernatant without disturbing resultant DNA pellet.
Carefully pour off or pipette out the supernatant, being careful not to disturb/lose the DNA pellet. The DNA pellet at this stage is very loose and difficult to see.
Repeat Steps 16 and 17.

After the final wash, carefully remove as much of the remaining ethanol as possible. You can use a fine-tipped pipette to aspirate small droplets. If you can't remove all the ethanol, that is ok. It is better to leave some ethanol than risk sucking up your DNA!

Air-dry the DNA pellet at room temperature in a laminar flow hood with the motor on for 00:10:00 - 00:20:00 or in a dry bath/heat block at 37 °C for 00:05:00 - 00:10:00 , until all visible ethanol has evaporated. The pellet might become translucent. Do not over-dry, as this can make the DNA difficult to resuspend.
Preheat 1x TE buffer ( pH 8.0 ) at 65 °C for 00:10:00 . This helps the DNA dissolve faster.
Add 50-100 µL of the preheated TE buffer to the DNA pellet.
Gently tap the tube or flick it to resuspend the DNA. Avoid vigorous vortexing, which can shear long DNA molecules.
Store the DNA at 4 °C for short-term use or at -20 °C for long-term storage.
Optional, if low cell yield. Onto the same tube, repeat steps 2 and 3.
Optional RNase Treatment . Add 5.0 µL RNase A ( 10 mg/mL ) to the suspension. Vortex to mix well. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:15:00 .
Add 150 µL freshly prepared lysis buffer. Vortex vigorously to mix well. Incubate at 37 °C for 00:30:00 .
Incubate On ice for 00:30:00 , or even longer (e.g. Overnight at -20 °C ) for better yield. Cold temperature enhances DNA precipitation.

Add 500 µL ice-cold absolute ethanol. Mix gently by inverting the tube slowly until DNA strings become visible (often looks like fine white threads). This may take several inversions. (If DNA concentration is low, the DNA strings may not be visible). Pause Point: You can stop at this point, store your samples at –20°C, and complete the procedure later.
Mix again gently by inverting the tube a few times. Centrifuge at 14000 rpm, °C, for 00:03:00 . A visible white DNA pellet should form at the bottom or side of the tube.
The DNA should dissolve, forming a clear solution. Centrifuge at 5000 rpm, °C, for 00:00:10 to pool the elution at the bottom of the tube.
